title: Carbide
problem: Carson's whole pitch is "the UI is generative." But every artifact on usecarson.com - the prospect pane, the deck editor, the workflow builder - looks like a pre designed React surface that the LLM populates, not a layout the model invents per task. That's the gap between the marketing and the reality.
direction: A typed, auditable generative UI runtime for agentic apps - every rendered component carries a capability scope, every re render is a stable diff, and every action is logged with a cryptographic intent trail.
why: The judgement of which problem. Carson's competitive advantage is "we can sell agentic UIs to regulated enterprises because we're ex Palantir." That advantage evaporates the first time a customer asks "prove the model can't escalate." carbide is the proof.

archetypes.name: carson evidence replay
archetypes.trigger: carson signal changes while whole context is stale
archetypes.expected: block release until cited evidence is regenerated

archetypes.name: whole boundary probe
archetypes.trigger: whole handoff crosses a policy or trust boundary
archetypes.expected: route to reviewer with evidence packet

archetypes.name: pitch regression harness
archetypes.trigger: pitch behavior regresses against the last accepted fixture
archetypes.expected: open a regression issue with trace and benchmark delta

archetypes.name: generative operator packet
archetypes.trigger: generative output needs a human-readable audit packet
archetypes.expected: accept only if decision claims cite fixture evidence
